message:
Add real clipboard tests with CI skip for copy command

Adds real clipboard round-trip tests that verify:
- Actual clipboard copy operation works
- Content can be read back via platform tools
- Character count matches reported value

Tests automatically skip in CI environments or when clipboard
tools aren't available (no display server). Uses platform-specific
detection for pbcopy/pbpaste (macOS), clip.exe/Get-Clipboard (Windows),
and wl-paste/xclip (Linux with display server).

Closes jeffreysprompts.com-12wj

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","title":"Implement theme provider and dark mode toggle","description":"**Details:**
- ThemeProvider wrapping app for dark/light mode
- Persists preference to localStorage
- Respects system preference on first visit
- Dark mode toggle in Nav component
- CSS variables switch based on theme

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
'use client';
import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react';

type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'system';

export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useState<Theme>('system');
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const stored = localStorage.getItem('theme') as Theme;
    if (stored) setTheme(stored);
  }, []);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const root = document.documentElement;
    const isDark = theme === 'dark' || 
      (theme === 'system' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches);
    root.classList.toggle('dark', isDark);
    localStorage.setItem('theme', theme);
  }, [theme]);
  
  return (
    <ThemeContext.Provider value={{ theme, setTheme }}>
      {children}
    </ThemeContext.Provider>
  );
}
```

**Toggle Component:**
- Sun/Moon icon button
- Cycles through light → dark → system
- Tooltip showing current mode

**Location:** 
- apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx

**Reasoning:** Dark mode is expected in developer tools. System preference respect shows attention to detail. Persistent preference provides consistent UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:00.527000897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ThemeProvider with light/dark/system modes, ThemeToggle component, integrated into Providers.tsx. +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4q8k","title":"EPIC: CLI Authentication & Premium Integration","description":"## Overview
Add optional authentication to the `jfp` CLI tool, allowing users to seamlessly access their premium account data including custom prompts, collections, saved/bookmarked prompts, and notes.

## Why This Matters
The CLI is the primary interface for power users and AI agents. By adding authentication:
- Premium users get their full library in the CLI
- Users can access their collections and saved prompts
- The "swap meet" community prompts become available
- Notes and annotations sync across devices
- Search spans all content (public + personal)

## Design Principles

### 1. Transparent for Non-Users
- All existing functionality works WITHOUT login
- No nag screens, no forced auth, no degraded experience
- Login is purely additive - it unlocks MORE, never restricts
- Clear, honest messaging about what premium provides

### 2. Works Everywhere
- Local machines: Browser-based OAuth with auto-callback
- Remote/SSH: Device code flow (6-character code)
- Headless servers: Same device code flow
- CI/CD: Token-based auth via environment variable

### 3. Secure & Private
- Tokens stored securely in user's config directory
- Short-lived access tokens with refresh capability
- No passwords stored locally
- Easy revocation via web dashboard

## Authentication Flows

### Flow 1: Local Machine (Default)
```
User runs: jfp login
    ↓
CLI starts local HTTP server on random port (e.g., 19847)
    ↓
CLI opens browser to: https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com/cli/auth?port=19847
    ↓
User completes Google OAuth on premium site
    ↓
Premium site redirects to: http://localhost:19847/callback?token=xxx
    ↓
CLI receives token, stores it, shows success message
    ↓
Local server shuts down
```

### Flow 2: Remote Machine (--remote or auto-detected)
```
User runs: jfp login --remote  (or CLI detects no display)
    ↓
CLI requests device code from: POST /api/cli/device-code
    ↓
API returns: { device_code: "abc123", user_code: "XYZW-1234", verification_url: "..." }
    ↓
CLI shows: "Visit https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com/cli/verify and enter code: XYZW-1234"
    ↓
User visits URL, logs in with Google, enters code
    ↓
Meanwhile, CLI polls: POST /api/cli/device-token with device_code
    ↓
Once user completes, API returns access token
    ↓
CLI stores token, shows success
```

### Flow 3: CI/CD / Programmatic
```
User sets: JFP_TOKEN=xxx in environment
    ↓
CLI detects env var, uses it for all authenticated requests
    ↓
No interactive login needed
```

## New CLI Commands

### `jfp login`
- Initiates authentication flow
- Options: `--remote` (force device code flow)
- Stores credentials in `~/.config/jfp/credentials.json`

### `jfp logout`
- Clears stored credentials
- Optionally revokes token on server

### `jfp whoami`
- Shows current authentication status
- Displays: email, tier, token expiry

### `jfp list --mine`
- Lists only user's custom prompts

### `jfp list --saved`
- Lists user's bookmarked/saved prompts

### `jfp collections`
- Lists user's collections
- `jfp collections <name>` shows prompts in collection

### `jfp notes <prompt-id>`
- Shows notes for a prompt
- `jfp notes <prompt-id> --add "note text"` adds a note

## API Endpoints Needed (Premium Site)

### Auth Endpoints
- `POST /api/cli/device-code` - Initiate device code flow
- `POST /api/cli/device-token` - Exchange device code for token
- `POST /api/cli/token/refresh` - Refresh access token
- `POST /api/cli/token/revoke` - Revoke token (logout)

### Data Endpoints (Authenticated)
- `GET /api/cli/me` - Current user info
- `GET /api/cli/prompts` - All prompts (public + user's)
- `GET /api/cli/prompts/mine` - User's custom prompts only
- `GET /api/cli/prompts/saved` - User's saved/bookmarked prompts
- `GET /api/cli/collections` - User's collections
- `GET /api/cli/collections/:id` - Collection details with prompts
- `GET /api/cli/notes/:promptId` - Notes for a prompt
- `POST /api/cli/notes/:promptId` - Add note to prompt

## Token Format
Using JWT with claims:
- `sub`: User ID
- `email`: User email
- `tier`: Subscription tier (free/premium)
- `exp`: Expiration time
- `iat`: Issued at time

## Credential Storage
Location: `~/.config/jfp/credentials.json`
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJ...",
  "refresh_token": "xxx",
  "expires_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "tier": "premium"
}
```

## Success Criteria
- `jfp login` works on local machines with browser
- `jfp login --remote` works on headless/SSH machines
- Authenticated users see their prompts in `jfp list`
- `jfp search` finds results in personal prompts
- Token refresh happens automatically
- Logout clears all credentials
- Non-authenticated users have identical experience to before","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:08:45.460885074-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:08:45.460885074-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-c8l","title":"Implement tokenizer (search/tokenize.ts)","description":"**Details:**
- `tokenize(text)` splits text into searchable tokens
- Lowercase normalization
- Punctuation removal (preserve hyphens in compound words)
- Stopword filtering (the, a, an, is, are, etc.)
- Optional stemming (simple suffix stripping)
- Unicode normalization (NFD→NFC)

**Implementation:**
```typescript
export function tokenize(text: string): string[] {
  return text
    .toLowerCase()
    .normalize('NFD')
    .replace(/[^\w\s-]/g, ' ')
    .split(/\s+/)
    .filter(token => token.length > 1 && !STOPWORDS.has(token));
}
```

**Stopwords:** Common English stopwords (50-100 words)

**Used By:** BM25 search, synonym expansion

**Reasoning:** Consistent tokenization is foundational for search quality. Must be fast (run on every keystroke in SpotlightSearch) and deterministic.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:32:51.034344375-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:18:07.171689686-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:18:07.171689686-05:00","close_reason":"Core package fully implemented with types, registry, bundles, workflows, schema, search (tokenize, synonyms, BM25, engine), export (skills, markdown, json), and template (render, variables)"}

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n299","title":"Implement device code flow for remote/SSH login","description":"## Task
Implement the device code flow for `jfp login --remote` that works on headless/SSH machines.

## Why
Many power users run the CLI on remote servers via SSH where there's no browser. The device code flow:
1. User runs `jfp login --remote` on server
2. CLI shows a URL and 6-character code
3. User visits URL on ANY device (phone, laptop, etc.)
4. User enters code after authenticating
5. CLI receives token via polling

This is the same pattern used by:
- GitHub CLI (`gh auth login`)
- Azure CLI (`az login`)
- AWS SSO (`aws sso login`)

## Implementation

### Add to `packages/cli/src/commands/login.ts`

```typescript
const POLL_INTERVAL = 2_000;  // Poll every 2 seconds
const MAX_POLL_ATTEMPTS = 60;  // 2 minutes max

interface DeviceCodeResponse {
  device_code: string;      // Internal code for polling
  user_code: string;        // Code user enters (e.g., "XYZW-1234")
  verification_url: string; // URL to visit
  expires_in: number;       // Seconds until expiry
  interval: number;         // Recommended poll interval
}

interface DeviceTokenResponse {
  access_token: string;
  refresh_token?: string;
  expires_at: string;
  email: string;
  tier: "free" | "premium";
  user_id: string;
}

interface DeviceTokenError {
  error: string;
  error_description?: string;
}

async function loginRemote(options: LoginOptions): Promise<void> {
  console.log(chalk.dim("Initiating device code authentication...\n"));
  
  // Request device code from API
  const deviceCodeResponse = await fetch(`${PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/device-code`, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify({ client_id: "jfp-cli" }),
  });
  
  if (!deviceCodeResponse.ok) {
    console.log(chalk.red("Failed to initiate authentication"));
    process.exit(1);
  }
  
  const { device_code, user_code, verification_url, interval } = 
    await deviceCodeResponse.json() as DeviceCodeResponse;
  
  // Display instructions to user
  console.log(boxen(
    `To sign in, visit:\n\n` +
    `  ${chalk.cyan(verification_url)}\n\n` +
    `And enter code:\n\n` +
    `  ${chalk.bold.yellow(formatUserCode(user_code))}`,
    { 
      padding: 1, 
      margin: 1, 
      borderStyle: "round",
      title: "Device Authentication",
      titleAlignment: "center",
    }
  ));
  
  console.log(chalk.dim("\nWaiting for authentication..."));
  console.log(chalk.dim("(Press Ctrl+C to cancel)\n"));
  
  // Poll for token
  const pollInterval = Math.max(interval * 1000, POLL_INTERVAL);
  let attempts = 0;
  
  while (attempts < MAX_POLL_ATTEMPTS) {
    await sleep(pollInterval);
    attempts++;
    
    try {
      const tokenResponse = await fetch(`${PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/device-token`, {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ 
          device_code,
          client_id: "jfp-cli",
        }),
      });
      
      if (tokenResponse.ok) {
        const credentials = await tokenResponse.json() as DeviceTokenResponse;
        
        // Save credentials
        await saveCredentials({
          access_token: credentials.access_token,
          refresh_token: credentials.refresh_token,
          expires_at: credentials.expires_at,
          email: credentials.email,
          tier: credentials.tier,
          user_id: credentials.user_id,
        });
        
        console.log(boxen(
          `${chalk.green("✓")} Logged in as ${chalk.bold(credentials.email)}\n` +
          `Tier: ${chalk.cyan(credentials.tier)}`,
          { padding: 1, margin: 1, borderStyle: "round", borderColor: "green" }
        ));
        
        return;
      }
      
      // Check for specific errors
      const errorBody = await tokenResponse.json() as DeviceTokenError;
      
      if (errorBody.error === "authorization_pending") {
        // User hasn't completed auth yet, keep polling
        process.stdout.write(".");
        continue;
      }
      
      if (errorBody.error === "slow_down") {
        // API wants us to slow down
        await sleep(5000);
        continue;
      }
      
      if (errorBody.error === "expired_token") {
        console.log(chalk.red("\n\nDevice code expired. Please try again."));
        process.exit(1);
      }
      
      if (errorBody.error === "access_denied") {
        console.log(chalk.red("\n\nAuthentication was denied."));
        process.exit(1);
      }
      
      // Unknown error
      console.log(chalk.red("\n\nAuthentication failed:"), errorBody.error_description || errorBody.error);
      process.exit(1);
      
    } catch (err) {
      // Network error, keep trying
      process.stdout.write("x");
    }
  }
  
  console.log(chalk.red("\n\nAuthentication timed out. Please try again."));
  process.exit(1);
}

function formatUserCode(code: string): string {
  // Format as "XXXX-XXXX" for easier reading
  if (code.length === 8 && !code.includes("-")) {
    return `${code.slice(0, 4)}-${code.slice(4)}`;
  }
  return code;
}

function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
  return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
}
```

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n3p","title":"Create build-data.ts script for data pipeline","description":"**Details:**
- Main build script that generates all static data files
- Validates prompts against schema
- Generates registry.json and registry.manifest.json
- Computes checksums for cache validation
- Reports errors with clear messages

**Script Responsibilities:**
1. Import prompts from packages/core
2. Validate all prompts against Zod schema
3. Generate registry.json with full payload
4. Generate registry.manifest.json with checksums
5. Write files to apps/web/public/
6. Output summary statistics

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// scripts/build-data.ts
import { prompts, bundles, workflows } from '@jeffreysprompts/core/prompts';
import { promptSchema } from '@jeffreysprompts/core/prompts/schema';
import { createHash } from 'crypto';

async function main() {
  console.log('Building data assets...');
  
  // Validate prompts
  const errors: string[] = [];
  for (const prompt of prompts) {
    const result = promptSchema.safeParse(prompt);
    if (!result.success) {
      errors.push(`${prompt.id}: ${result.error.message}`);
    }
  }
  
  if (errors.length) {
    console.error('Validation errors:', errors);
    process.exit(1);
  }
  
  // Build registry payload
  const registry = {
    schemaVersion: 1,
    version: process.env.JFP_REGISTRY_VERSION ?? '0.0.0',
    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    prompts,
    bundles,
    workflows,
    meta: {
      totalPromptCount: prompts.length,
      categories: [...new Set(prompts.map(p => p.category))],
      tags: [...new Set(prompts.flatMap(p => p.tags))].sort()
    }
  };
  
  // Compute checksums
  const registryJson = JSON.stringify(registry);
  const registryHash = createHash('sha256').update(registryJson).digest('hex');
  
  const manifest = {
    version: registry.version,
    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    checksums: {
      registry: `sha256:${registryHash}`,
      prompts: Object.fromEntries(
        prompts.map(p => [p.id, `sha256:${createHash('sha256').update(p.content).digest('hex')}`])
      )
    }
  };
  
  // Write files
  await Bun.write('apps/web/public/registry.json', registryJson);
  await Bun.write('apps/web/public/registry.manifest.json', JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2));
  
  console.log(`✓ Generated registry.json (${prompts.length} prompts)`);
  console.log(`✓ Generated registry.manifest.json`);
}

main();
```

**Run in CI:**
- Part of build step before Next.js build
- Fails CI if validation errors

**Location:** scripts/build-data.ts

**Reasoning:** Centralized data pipeline ensures registry consistency. interface JfpConfig {

```typescript
  };

```

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// packages/cli/src/lib/config.ts

const CONFIG_PATH = join(homedir(), '.config', 'jfp', 'config.json');

const DEFAULT_CONFIG: JfpConfig = {
  registry: {
    url: 'https://jeffreysprompts.com',
    cacheTtl: 3600,
    autoRefresh: true,
  },
  skills: {
    personalDir: join(homedir(), '.config', 'claude', 'skills'),
    projectDir: '.claude/skills',
    preferProject: false,
  },
  output: {
    color: true,
    json: false,
  },
  updates: {
    autoCheck: true,
    autoUpdate: false,
  },
};

export function loadConfig(): JfpConfig {
  try {
    const raw = readFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, 'utf-8');
    return { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...JSON.parse(raw) };
  } catch {
    return DEFAULT_CONFIG;
  }
}

export function saveConfig(config: Partial<JfpConfig>): void {
  const current = loadConfig();
  const merged = { ...current, ...config };
  mkdirSync(dirname(CONFIG_PATH), { recursive: true });
  writeFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, JSON.stringify(merged, null, 2));
}
```

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tlz1","title":"Implement jfp login command with local browser flow","description":"## Task
Implement the `jfp login` command that authenticates users via browser-based OAuth flow for local machines.

## Why
This is the primary authentication method for users running the CLI on their local machine with a browser available. It provides a seamless experience:
1. User runs `jfp login`
2. Browser opens automatically
3. User clicks "Sign in with Google"
4. CLI automatically receives token
5. Done!

## File: `packages/cli/src/commands/login.ts`

### Implementation

```typescript
import { createServer, type IncomingMessage, type ServerResponse } from "http";
import { URL } from "url";
import open from "open";
import chalk from "chalk";
import boxen from "boxen";
import { saveCredentials, loadCredentials, type Credentials } from "../lib/credentials";

const PREMIUM_URL = process.env.JFP_PREMIUM_URL ?? "https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com";
const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 120_000;  // 2 minutes

interface LoginOptions {
  remote?: boolean;
  timeout?: number;
}

export async function loginCommand(options: LoginOptions = {}): Promise<void> {
  // Check if already logged in
  const existing = await loadCredentials();
  if (existing) {
    console.log(chalk.yellow("You are already logged in as " + existing.email));
    console.log(chalk.dim("Run 'jfp logout' first to switch accounts"));
    return;
  }
  
  // Force remote flow if requested or if no display available
  if (options.remote || !canOpenBrowser()) {
    return loginRemote(options);
  }
  
  return loginLocal(options);
}

function canOpenBrowser(): boolean {
  // Check if we're in an environment where we can open a browser
  // SSH sessions typically don't have DISPLAY set
  if (process.env.SSH_CLIENT || process.env.SSH_TTY) {
    return false;
  }
  
  // Check for display on Linux
  if (process.platform === "linux" && !process.env.DISPLAY && !process.env.WAYLAND_DISPLAY) {
    return false;
  }
  
  return true;
}

async function loginLocal(options: LoginOptions): Promise<void> {
  const timeout = options.timeout ?? DEFAULT_TIMEOUT;
  
  console.log(chalk.dim("Starting login flow..."));
  
  // Start local server to receive callback
  const { port, tokenPromise, close } = await startCallbackServer(timeout);
  
  // Build auth URL
  const authUrl = new URL(`${PREMIUM_URL}/cli/auth`);
  authUrl.searchParams.set("port", String(port));
  authUrl.searchParams.set("redirect", "local");
  
  console.log(chalk.dim("\nOpening browser to sign in with Google..."));
  console.log(chalk.dim(`URL: ${authUrl.toString()}\n`));
  
  // Open browser
  try {
    await open(authUrl.toString());
  } catch {
    console.log(chalk.yellow("Could not open browser automatically."));
    console.log(`Please visit: ${chalk.cyan(authUrl.toString())}`);
  }
  
  // Wait for callback
  console.log(chalk.dim("Waiting for authentication..."));
  
  try {
    const credentials = await tokenPromise;
    close();
    
    // Save credentials
    await saveCredentials(credentials);
    
    console.log(boxen(
      `${chalk.green("✓")} Logged in as ${chalk.bold(credentials.email)}\n` +
      `Tier: ${chalk.cyan(credentials.tier)}`,
      { padding: 1, margin: 1, borderStyle: "round", borderColor: "green" }
    ));
    
  } catch (err) {
    close();
    
    if (err instanceof Error && err.message === "timeout") {
      console.log(chalk.red("\nLogin timed out. Please try again."));
      console.log(chalk.dim("Tip: Use 'jfp login --remote' for headless environments"));
    } else {
      console.log(chalk.red("\nLogin failed:"), err instanceof Error ? err.message : err);
    }
    process.exit(1);
  }
}

interface CallbackServer {
  port: number;
  tokenPromise: Promise<Credentials>;
  close: () => void;
}

async function startCallbackServer(timeoutMs: number): Promise<CallbackServer> {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    let resolveToken: (creds: Credentials) => void;
    let rejectToken: (err: Error) => void;
    
    const tokenPromise = new Promise<Credentials>((res, rej) => {
      resolveToken = res;
      rejectToken = rej;
    });
    
    const server = createServer((req: IncomingMessage, res: ServerResponse) => {
      const url = new URL(req.url!, `http://localhost`);
      
      if (url.pathname === "/callback") {
        // Parse token from query params
        const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
        const email = url.searchParams.get("email");
        const tier = url.searchParams.get("tier") as "free" | "premium";
        const expiresAt = url.searchParams.get("expires_at");
        const userId = url.searchParams.get("user_id");
        const refreshToken = url.searchParams.get("refresh_token");
        const error = url.searchParams.get("error");
        
        if (error) {
          res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "text/html" });
          res.end(errorPage(error));
          rejectToken(new Error(error));
          return;
        }
        
        if (!token || !email || !tier || !userId) {
          res.writeHead(400, { "Content-Type": "text/html" });
          res.end(errorPage("Invalid callback parameters"));
          rejectToken(new Error("Invalid callback parameters"));
          return;
        }
        
        // Success!
        res.writeHead(200, { "Content-Type": "text/html" });
        res.end(successPage(email));
        
        resolveToken({
          access_token: token,
          refresh_token: refreshToken ?? undefined,
          expires_at: expiresAt ?? new Date(Date.now() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(),
          email,
          tier,
          user_id: userId,
        });
      } else {
        res.writeHead(404);
        res.end("Not found");
      }
    });
    
    // Find available port
    server.listen(0, "127.0.0.1", () => {
      const addr = server.address();
      const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : 0;
      
      // Set timeout
      const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {
        rejectToken(new Error("timeout"));
      }, timeoutMs);
      
      resolve({
        port,
        tokenPromise: tokenPromise.finally(() => clearTimeout(timeoutId)),
        close: () => server.close(),
      });
    });
    
    server.on("error", reject);
  });
}
```

Create a credential storage module that securely stores and retrieves authentication tokens for the CLI.

## Why
This is foundational for all auth features. We need:
- Secure storage location following XDG Base Directory spec
- Atomic file operations (no partial writes)
- Token expiry checking
- Graceful handling of missing/corrupt credentials

## File: `packages/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts`

### Implementation

```typescript
import { homedir } from "os";
import { join } from "path";
import { readFile, writeFile, mkdir, unlink } from "fs/promises";
import { existsSync } from "fs";

export interface Credentials {
  access_token: string;
  refresh_token?: string;
  expires_at: string;  // ISO 8601
  email: string;
  tier: "free" | "premium";
  user_id: string;
}

// XDG Base Directory compliant path
function getCredentialsPath(): string {
  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME;
  const configDir = xdgConfig || join(homedir(), ".config");
  return join(configDir, "jfp", "credentials.json");
}

function getConfigDir(): string {
  const credPath = getCredentialsPath();
  return credPath.substring(0, credPath.lastIndexOf("/"));
}

/**
 * Load credentials from disk
 * Returns null if not logged in or credentials are invalid
 */
export async function loadCredentials(): Promise<Credentials | null> {
  const path = getCredentialsPath();
  
  if (!existsSync(path)) {
    return null;
  }
  
  try {
    const content = await readFile(path, "utf-8");
    const creds = JSON.parse(content) as Credentials;
    
    // Basic validation
    if (!creds.access_token || !creds.email) {
      return null;
    }
    
    return creds;
  } catch {
    // Corrupt file or parse error
    return null;
  }
}

/**
 * Check if credentials are expired
 */
export function isExpired(creds: Credentials): boolean {
  if (!creds.expires_at) return false;
  
  const expiresAt = new Date(creds.expires_at);
  const now = new Date();
  
  // Consider expired 5 minutes before actual expiry (buffer for clock skew)
  const bufferMs = 5 * 60 * 1000;
  return now.getTime() > expiresAt.getTime() - bufferMs;
}

/**
 * Save credentials to disk
 * Atomic write with temp file to prevent corruption
 */
export async function saveCredentials(creds: Credentials): Promise<void> {
  const path = getCredentialsPath();
  const dir = getConfigDir();
  
  // Ensure config directory exists
  if (!existsSync(dir)) {
    await mkdir(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
  }
  
  // Write atomically via temp file
  const tempPath = `${path}.tmp`;
  const content = JSON.stringify(creds, null, 2);
  
  await writeFile(tempPath, content, { mode: 0o600 });  // User read/write only
  await rename(tempPath, path);
}

/**
 * Clear stored credentials (logout)
 */
export async function clearCredentials(): Promise<void> {
  const path = getCredentialsPath();
  
  if (existsSync(path)) {
    await unlink(path);
  }
}

/**
 * Get access token for API calls
 * Returns null if not logged in
 * Handles environment variable override (for CI/CD)
 */
export async function getAccessToken(): Promise<string | null> {
  // Environment variable takes precedence (for CI/CD)
  const envToken = process.env.JFP_TOKEN;
  if (envToken) {
    return envToken;
  }
  
  const creds = await loadCredentials();
  if (!creds) {
    return null;
  }
  
  // TODO: Auto-refresh if expired (implement in later task)
  if (isExpired(creds)) {
    return null;  // Force re-login for now
  }
  
  return creds.access_token;
}

/**
 * Check if user is currently logged in
 */
export async function isLoggedIn(): Promise<boolean> {
  const token = await getAccessToken();
  return token !== null;
}
```

## Security Considerations
- File permissions: 0o600 (user read/write only)
- Directory permissions: 0o700 (user only)
- Atomic writes prevent partial/corrupt files
- No sensitive data in error messages
- Environment variable override for CI/CD (no file storage needed)

```typescript
// apps/web/src/contexts/basket-context.tsx
'use client';

interface BasketContextValue {
  items: string[]; // prompt IDs
  addItem: (id: string) => void;
  removeItem: (id: string) => void;
  clearBasket: () => void;
  isInBasket: (id: string) => boolean;
}

export const BasketContext = createContext<BasketContextValue | null>(null);

export function BasketProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  const [items, setItems] = useLocalStorage<string[]>('jfp-basket', []);
  
  const addItem = (id: string) => {
    setItems(prev => prev.includes(id) ? prev : [...prev, id]);
  };
  
  const removeItem = (id: string) => {
    setItems(prev => prev.filter(i => i !== id));
  };
  
  const clearBasket = () => setItems([]);
  
  const isInBasket = (id: string) => items.includes(id);
  
  return (
    <BasketContext.Provider value={{ items, addItem, removeItem, clearBasket, isInBasket }}>
      {children}
    </BasketContext.Provider>
  );
}

// apps/web/src/hooks/use-basket.ts
export function useBasket() {
  const context = useContext(BasketContext);
  if (!context) throw new Error('useBasket must be used within BasketProvider');
  return context;
}
```
